title: The Prime Day Vacuum Deals That Don't Suck (Unless It's Dirt)
author: Nena Farrell
text: Amazon Prime Day June 2026Absolute Best Amazon Prime Day DealsApple Watch DealsHeadphones DealsMesh Wi-Fi Router DealsOura Ring DealsVacuum Cleaner DealsNow ReadingBeauty Tech DealsWalmart DealsDeals on Power BanksSmart Bird Feeder DealsDeals Under $30Action Camera DealsAll products featured on WIRED are independently selected by our editors. However, we may receive compensation from retailers and/or from purchases of products through these links. Learn more.The summer sale extravaganza has come early this year, and while this is one of many sale events you'll see between now and the holidays, these Prime Day vacuum deals are worth taking a look at.There's an impressive range of vacuums on sale, from some of my favorite cordless vacuums and Dyson powerhouses, to robot vacuums and handheld vacuums. You can get my favorite cordless vacuum, the Shark PowerDetect ($300) for $100 cheaper than usual, or the already affordable Bissell PowerClean FurFinder ($150) with an extra discount. The best robot vacuum we've tried, the Shark PowerDetect UV Reveal ($850) also has a fantastic price tag this week for how powerful it is, and you can get great wet vacuums like the Narwal S30 Pro ($330) on sale too.All of these models and more that we've tested and love are on sale right now. Here are all the Prime Day vacuum deals you don't want to miss. Also be sure to browse our roundup of the Absolute Best Prime Day Deals.Updated June 24, 2026: We've updated these deals with the latest prices, and added deals on more vacuums including the Narwal S30 Pro, Dyson Spot+Scrub Ai, and iRobot Roomba 705 Combo, and added a new section about deals on hand vacuums.The Best Prime Day Vacuum DealsBest Cordless Vacuum DealsBest Robot Vacuum DealsBest Hand Vacuum DealsMore Deals I've SpottedBest Cordless Vacuum DealsPhotograph: Nena FarrellPhotograph: Nena FarrellCourtesy of BissellBissellPowerClean FurFinder$260 $150 (42% off) AmazonThe whole reason to shop for a vacuum on Prime Day is to get a great deal. The Bissell PowerClean FurFinder is already a great price for a cordless vacuum with its power and attachments, and it's often on sale at this low price. It has 200W of power, a bright headlight to spot dust and debris, a handful of attachments including the FurFinder head for sucking up pet hair, and a HEPA filtration system. It can stand up on its own, too, which comes in handy.Most PopularGearThe 129 Absolute Best Prime Day DealsBy Louryn StrampeHow To and Advice28 Tips to Take Your ChatGPT Prompts to the Next LevelBy David NieldSmart HomeThe Best Prime Day Tech Deals on the Gadgets You Actually WantBy Simon HillDealsThe 12 Best Apple Deals to Snag During Amazon Prime DayBy Louryn StrampePhotograph: Nena FarrellPhotograph: Nena FarrellPhotograph: Nena FarrellSharkPowerDetect Cordless Stick Vacuum$450 $300 (33% off) Amazon$450 $300 (33% off) SharkNinjaMy all-time favorite cordless vacuum is also on sale. The Shark PowerDetect is a popular cordless vacuum for a reason, from its powerful suction, bendable neck, large dust cup that's easy to empty, and HEPA filtration. This vacuum outperformed a Dyson in my tests with sand and cereal on carpet, and works great as a hand vacuum, too. It's already a well-priced vacuum considering all it includes, and it's even better now with this $100 discount during Prime Day.Photograph: Nena FarrellPhotograph: Nena FarrellPhotograph: Best BuyDysonGen5Detect$1,050 $660 (37% off) Amazon$1,050 $800 (24% off) Best Buy$1,050 $800 (24% off) DysonThis Dyson is not only powerful, but now, it has a fantastic price too. I usually don't see it for this cheap, but now that Dyson launched new vacuum models, it's likely going to have a better sale price going forward. So, while the Dyson Gen5Detect isn't technically the top-of-the-line model anymore, it's still impressively powerful with 280AW of suction and has a brighter laser than Dyson's older models. It also has a true power button rather than the trigger button that models like the V15 have. The addition of the HEPA filtration and its powerful suction makes it a go-to option for homes with carpet and pet hair.Most PopularGearThe 129 Absolute Best Prime Day DealsBy Louryn StrampeHow To and Advice28 Tips to Take Your ChatGPT Prompts to the Next LevelBy David NieldSmart HomeThe Best Prime Day Tech Deals on the Gadgets You Actually WantBy Simon HillDealsThe 12 Best Apple Deals to Snag During Amazon Prime DayBy Louryn StrampePhotograph: Nena FarrellPhotograph: Nena FarrellPhotograph: Nena FarrellPhotograph: Nena FarrellNarwalS30 Pro$550 $335 (39% off) Amazon$550 $335 (39% off) NarwalIf you're looking for a better mop experience, try a cordless wet dry vacuum. The Narwal S30 Pro makes mopping my massive main floor a breeze. You fill up the clean water tank in the head of the wet vac, and then it filters dirty water and debris up into the dirty water tank in the body of the device. You can add a little disposable basket to catch large debris so that it doesn't get too murky, and it was much easier to clean than the Dyson WashG1. It's a great purchase for homes with primarily hard floors, especially at this discounted price.Best Robot Vacuum DealsPhotograph: Nena FarrellPhotograph: Nena FarrellPhotograph: Nena FarrellVideo: Nena FarrellSharkPowerDetect UV Reveal$1,300 $850 (35% off) Amazon$1,300 $850 (35% off) SharkNinjaI don't like AI being forced into products unnecessarily, but I have to admit this robot vacuum's built-in AI features impressed me. The Shark PowerDetect UV Reveal uses a UV light and a camera to spot stains, and the AI will analyze the footage to find where it should go to scrub the floors. Once the initial clean is complete, it will announce to you that it's found stains and is headed back out to “viciously attack them.” It also does a good job on hard floors, carpets, and rugs alike, but sadly can only learn one floor at a time. Still, I've yet to be this impressed with any other robot vacuum's cleaning job, especially for the price (even more so while on sale!)Most PopularGearThe 129 Absolute Best Prime Day DealsBy Louryn StrampeHow To and Advice28 Tips to Take Your ChatGPT Prompts to the Next LevelBy David NieldSmart HomeThe Best Prime Day Tech Deals on the Gadgets You Actually WantBy Simon HillDealsThe 12 Best Apple Deals to Snag During Amazon Prime DayBy Louryn StrampePhotograph: Adrienne SoDreameX50 Ultra$1,050 $850 (19% off) Amazon$1,600 $1,000 (38% off) DreameThis is another great robot vacuum with AI that you'll actually be impressed with. Former WIRED reviewer Adrienne So found the Dreame X50 Ultra would track and recognize spots in her home that would get the dirtiest (for her, it was in front of her dog's bed) and remind her to run the vacuum to clean them. It also has side wands for edge cleaning and a large capacity docking station for all that debris it's reminding you to let it clean. It's got a nice discount as well right now for Prime Day.Photograph: Nena FarrellPhotograph: Nena FarrellPhotograph: Nena FarrellDysonSpot+Scrub Ai$1,200 $850 (29% off) Amazon$1,200 DysonIf you want another AI-powered robot vacuum option, Dyson's newest robot vacuum also uses artificial intelligence to spot where it needs to scrub. No wonder it's named the Dyson Spot+Scrub Ai. I wasn't as impressed by the AI as I was with the Shark UV Reveal AI, but it still did a great job both mopping and vacuuming my floors. Its able to learn multiple maps, and has one of the largest base stations I've ever seen that looks distinctly Dyson with its clear dustbin.Most PopularGearThe 129 Absolute Best Prime Day DealsBy Louryn StrampeHow To and Advice28 Tips to Take Your ChatGPT Prompts to the Next LevelBy David NieldSmart HomeThe Best Prime Day Tech Deals on the Gadgets You Actually WantBy Simon HillDealsThe 12 Best Apple Deals to Snag During Amazon Prime DayBy Louryn StrampePhotograph: Nena FarrellPhotograph: Nena FarrellVideo: Nena FarrellEufyOmni C28$800 $450 (44% off) Amazon$800 $450 (44% off) EufyIf you want a cheaper robot vacuum without skimping on a good clean, the Eufy Omni C28 is my favorite budget robot vacuum. It's usually on sale for around $500, but it's on sale for $450 for Prime Day. Even though it's affordable, it still does a good job vacuuming and mopping (though it won't spot stains and messes on its own, you'll have to tell it when to do a deeper clean) and can learn multiple floors of your home.Courtesy of iRobotiRobotRoomba Max 705 Combo$1,300 $899 (31% off) AmazonI really like this vacuum, but I'll warn you now it was one of the longest to set up and took three tries. Once it was set up, though, the iRobot app is intuitive and makes it easy to follow along the route of your vacuum cleaning the rooms of your home, and looks pretty doing it with the nice finish that both the vacuum and base have.Best Hand Vacuum DealsPhotograph: Nena FarrellPhotograph: Nena FarrellCourtesy of Black & DeckerBlack & DeckerDustbuster Flex$130 $74 (43% off) Amazon$110 Home Depot$125 Black + DeckerThis is a great hand vacuum thanks to the charging stand and long hose that make it easy to use in cars (and especially deep trunks). The base keeps the vacuum charged (and thank goodness since it only has a 15 minute battery life, so you'll want an easy charging setup) and stores its attachments, too.Most PopularGearThe 129 Absolute Best Prime Day DealsBy Louryn StrampeHow To and Advice28 Tips to Take Your ChatGPT Prompts to the Next LevelBy David NieldSmart HomeThe Best Prime Day Tech Deals on the Gadgets You Actually WantBy Simon HillDealsThe 12 Best Apple Deals to Snag During Amazon Prime DayBy Louryn StrampePhotograph: Brenda StolyarCourtesy of DysonDysonCar+Boat$300 $229 (24% off) Amazon$300 $230 (23% off) Best Buy$300 $230 (23% off) DysonIf you're looking for a great hand vacuum, this Dyson model has impressive power and battery life for a handheld. It's one of the cheapest ways to get a Dyson vacuum into your home, although, Dyson stick vacuums can also double as a handheld. Still, it's a nice little discount on an already affordable (for a Dyson) vacuum.Photograph: Nena FarrellCourtesy of SharkSharkUltraCyclone Pet Pro Plus$100 $70 (30% off) Amazon$100 $70 (30% off) Best BuyThis little handheld vacuum is designed with pets and pet hair in mind. I really liked it for general household messes like Cheerios my toddler spilled or strange piles of dirt and sand he seemed to create everywhere he went. It has great suction with two cyclonic airstreams and a motorized power brush that works especially well on hair. The battery lasts about a week of sporadic use.More Deals I've SpottedI'll be looking for more great vacuum deals all week long. Here's a few more you might want to check out.Bissell PowerClean Cordless Stick Vacuum for $150 ($50 off): This is the cheaper version of the Bissell FurFinder vacuum since it's basically the same but with a weaker headlight and no FurFinder attachment, but it's discounted for the same price. I'd just get the FurFinder above since you'll get more without spending more.Dyson Ball Animal 3 Extra for $400 ($150 off): If for some reason you really, really want an upright vacuum instead of a cordless model, then Dyson's Ball Animal 3 Extra (Extra means it comes with twice the attachments compared to the regular Ball Animal 3) is on sale. It's designed with pet hair in mind, and former WIRED reviewer Jaina Grey was happy with how well it vacuumed her rabbit's hair around the house.Ecovacs Deebot X12 OmniCyclone for $800 ($600 off): Ecovacs is the parent company of both Deebot and Yeedi, but Yeedi is usually cheaper with some similar features. Still, we've liked past Deebots robot vacuums we've tried, and this is a good deal on one of its premium robot vacuums. I haven't tested it yet, but I like that you can mark spots in the app that are extra dirty so that the robot vacuum knows to use its pre-spray on the stains and mess before it starts mopping.Ecovacs Deebot T90 Pro Omni for $500 ($400 off): If you're looking for more options in the $500 range, the Deebot T90 Pro Omni is also on sale for Prime Day to bring it down into that range. It's usually on sale for $700, so this is a better deal than usual. It's another one I have yet to test, but promises a self-cleaning roller mop, edge cleaning, and impressive 30,000 Pa of suction.Power up with unlimited access to WIRED.
